decks.notes: This deck focuses on utilizing disruptive creatures and equipment to control the board while putting pressure on the opponent with aggressive creatures. The goal is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while using Aether Vial to cheat creatures into play and utilize powerful removal spells like Swords to Plowshares. The deck also utilizes powerful creature synergies and recursion to outvalue the opponent and overwhelm them with threats. The inclusion of Yorion as a companion provides additional value and flexibility in longer games.

The main focus of this UR Tempo aggro deck is to control the board and apply pressure on the opponent quickly. It utilizes efficient creatures like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler to deal early damage and disrupt the opponent's game plan with countermagic like Daze and Force of Will. The deck also includes a variety of cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to dig for threats and answers, along with removal spells like Lightning Bolt. The mana base is streamlined with fetchlands like Flooded Strand and Volcanic Island, along with Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base.

This deck aims to control the game through the manipulation of lands and spells, utilizing cards like Crop Rotation, Life from the Loam, and Exploration to establish a strong board presence. The key win condition revolves around assembling the powerful Dark Depths/Thespian's Stage combo to create a 20/20 Marit Lage token. The deck also includes disruptive elements such as Ghost Quarter and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Additionally, Boseiju, Who Endures allows for uncounterable spells, providing further control over the game. The deck is designed to grind out the opponent and secure victory through attrition.

The main concept of this deck is to build a powerful aggro strategy using artifacts and blue cards. Emry, Lurker of the Loch is a key card for recursion and card advantage, while cards like Goblin Welder and Urza's Saga help generate additional value. The deck includes control elements like Force of Will and Pithing Needle to disrupt opposing strategies. Overall, the deck aims to quickly develop a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent with efficient artifact creatures and powerful artifact synergies.

This deck focuses on using mana acceleration and card advantage to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It aims to ramp up quickly with cards like Gaea's Cradle and Green Sun's Zenith, while using powerful creatures like Allosaurus Shepherd and Craterhoof Behemoth to finish the game. It also includes disruption tools like Thoughtseize and Collector Ouphe to slow down the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck seeks to gain a strategic advantage early on and maintain control throughout the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing Eldrazi creatures and powerful colorless spells to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy. The key strategy revolves around playing Eldrazi creatures as quickly as possible using ramp from lands like Ancient Tomb and Eldrazi Temple, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer. The deck aims to close out games quickly and efficiently by using the large Eldrazi creatures to deal significant damage and control the board with powerful spells like Kozilek's Command.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently reanimate powerful creatures such as Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty using spells like Animate Dead and Reanimate. The deck also includes cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while cards like Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers provide additional utility. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disrupt their strategy to come out ahead in the game.

The main concept of the deck is to establish a prison-like control over the game using a combination of artifacts and powerful spells. By utilizing cards like Planar Nexus, Trinisphere, Karn, the Great Creator, and Ugin, Eye of the Storms, the deck seeks to restrict the opponent's options and effectively lock them out of the game. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos, Grim Monolith, and The One Ring provide additional utility and ramp capabilities. The deck aims to create a dominating board state while preventing the opponent from mounting a successful offense.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Agate Instigator, Guide of Souls, and Ocelot Pride. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah provides additional aggression and synergy with the deck's creatures. Cards like Goblin Bombardment and Voice of Victory offer additional reach and protection. The deck also includes disruption in the form of cards like Hexing Squelcher and Static Prison to control the opponent's game plan. The mana base is optimized with fetch lands like Arid Mesa and efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares to maintain tempo and pressure.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize Gaea's Cradle and various mana dorks to ramp up quickly and cast powerful creatures such as Atraxa, Grand Unifier, Craterhoof Behemoth, and Grist, the Hunger Tide. The deck also includes control elements such as Collector Ouphe and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Natural Order is a key card in this deck, allowing the player to search for a powerful creature and put it directly onto the battlefield. Overall, the main strategy is to generate a lot of mana and overwhelm the opponent with big threats.

This deck focuses on utilizing creatures with disruptive abilities to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy, while also taking advantage of combo synergies to generate card advantage and maintain tempo. The deck aims to apply pressure early, while also having ways to deal with threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It combines aggressive elements with disruptive elements to create a versatile and challenging gameplay experience for the opponent, while also having the potential to outvalue the opponent in the long game.

This deck focuses on deploying threats quickly and efficiently to gain control of the game early on. By utilizing fast mana sources like Ancient Tomb and Chrome Mox, the deck can power out aggressive creatures like Anointed Peacekeeper and Seasoned Dungeoneer. With disruptive elements like Chancellor of the Annex and Trinisphere, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining pressure on the board. The companion, Yorion, Sky Nomad, provides additional value and flexibility for the deck's strategy. Overall, the deck's goal is to establish dominance and close out games quickly.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent through resource denial with cards like Smallpox, Thoughtseize, and Wasteland, while also using control elements such as Ensnaring Bridge and Karn, the Great Creator. The archetype utilizes a combination of hand disruption, removal, and stax effects to slow down the opponent's game plan and gain incremental advantages over time. Additionally, the deck includes utility lands like Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale to further hinder the opponent's board development.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly ramp into playing powerful creatures such as Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Griselbrand by utilizing spells like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. By utilizing cards like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Omniscience, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats while also having control elements like Force of Will and Flusterstorm to protect its combo pieces. The key strategy is to cheat out big creatures onto the battlefield early and take advantage of their powerful effects to gain a decisive advantage.

The main concept of this deck is to maintain control over the game while dealing damage quickly and efficiently. By utilizing cards like Daze, Force of Will, and Lightning Bolt to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while using cards like Brainstorm and Ponder to draw into essential cards, this deck aims to keep the tempo in its favor. Dragon's Rage Channeler and Stormchaser's Talent provide additional threats that can quickly close out the game. With a mix of disruption, card advantage, and aggressive creatures, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

This deck is focused on controlling the game through card advantage, removal spells, and counterspells. By using cards like Swords to Plowshares, Force of Will, and Prismatic Ending to deal with threats, along with card draw spells like Brainstorm and Ponder, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and maintain control of the board. The inclusion of Teferi, Time Raveler and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides additional utility and card advantage, while cards like Murktide Regent and Wrath of the Skies serve as win conditions to finish off the game.
